statusLine: EU fuel stocks are under strain with 18 of 27 countries holding critical reserves in at least one fuel type, despite overall averages remaining above the 90-day minimum threshold.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as two-thirds of EU member states struggle with critically low reserves in key fuel categories. Romania presents the most severe situation with all three fuel types in critical status - petrol at just 43.6 days, diesel at 20.2 days, and jet fuel at 60 days, all well below the mandatory 90-day minimum. Poland and Spain also show critical levels across all categories, with Poland's jet fuel reserves particularly concerning at only 21.3 days.

Diesel shortages appear most widespread, with 15 of 27 countries reporting critical diesel stocks below 90 days. This includes major economies like Germany (63.9 days), France (83.4 days), and Italy (64.3 days). Jet fuel presents another vulnerability, with 19 countries below the threshold, though some maintain substantial reserves like Slovenia and Estonia at 365 days each.

The supply strain coincides with elevated oil prices, as Brent crude trades at $94.76 per barrel despite a recent 13.3% decline. Average fuel prices reflect this pressure, with diesel at €2.00 per liter and significant regional variations - Netherlands petrol costs €2.334 compared to Malta's €1.34. The data indicates recent strategic reserve releases responding to Middle East supply disruptions, explaining the current stock depletion across multiple member states.
keyPoints: 18 of 27 EU countries report critical fuel reserves in at least one category, with diesel most affected (15 countries below 90 days)
Romania faces the most severe shortage with all fuel types critical - diesel reserves at just 20.2 days pose immediate supply risk
Major economies including Germany, France, and Italy maintain critical diesel stocks well below the 90-day requirement
Jet fuel shortages span 19 countries, though strategic reserves in Slovenia and Estonia provide regional buffer capacity
Despite stock pressures, EU-wide averages remain above mandatory minimums: petrol 94.5 days, diesel 95.8 days, jet fuel 89.9 days
